edges.description: Mild congenital macular maldevelopment can present as grade 1 drusen-like macular lesions.
Mild-to-moderate macular developmental lesions can appear as patchy yellowish-white macular pigmentation changes.
More severe congenital macular maldevelopment can produce large areas of macular thinning or tissue loss.
Central macular lesions can impair central visual function and produce a central scotoma phenotype.

nodes.description: Patchy yellowish-white macular lesions represent abnormal macular pigmentation within the NCMD lesion spectrum.

evidence.snippet: All three patients had macular involvement ranging from patchy yellowish-white lesions to big-area thinning, which are typical for NCMD.

nodes.description: NCMD itself has no established disease-modifying therapy, but choroidal neovascularization can be treated with intravitreal anti-VEGF therapy.

evidence.snippet: The visual acuity improved from 20/400 to 20/150 in the right eye and from 20/100 to 20/40 in the left eye after intravitreal bevacizumab treatment for CNV.

nodes.description: Central scotoma can occur when macular lesions affect the fixation point.

evidence.snippet: The proband presented with lifelong bilateral vision impairment with bilaterally symmetric vitelliform Best disease-like appearing macular lesions.

nodes.description: Choroidal neovascularization is a vision-threatening complication of NCMD that can present with intraretinal and subretinal fluid and respond to anti-VEGF treatment.

evidence.snippet: Optical coherence tomography examination showed intraretinal and subretinal fluid consistent with CNV.

nodes.description: NCMD-associated noncoding variants cluster in regulatory elements near PRDM13, and tandem duplications can include PRDM13 or nearby DNase I hypersensitive regulatory sites. These variants alter retinal enhancer activity and dysregulate PRDM13 during central retinal development.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.snippet: Four of these strongly implicate the involvement of PRDM13 in macular development, whereas the pathophysiologic mechanism of the fifth remains unknown but may involve the developmental dysregulation of IRX1.
Overall, this study provides insight into the cis-regulatory mechanisms of NCMD and supports that this condition is a retinal enhanceropathy.

nodes.description: Dysregulated retinal developmental transcriptional control arrests or disrupts human macular development. The resulting lesions are congenital or present in early infancy and usually do not progress between clinical grades.

evidence.snippet: We identified 5 rare mutations, each capable of arresting human macular development.
All three patients had macular involvement ranging from patchy yellowish-white lesions to big-area thinning, which are typical for NCMD.

nodes.description: A distinct MCDR3 locus involves a large duplication including IRX1. The causal mechanism is less resolved than the PRDM13 hotspot mechanism, but current evidence supports developmental dysregulation of IRX1 as a second regulatory route to NCMD-like macular maldevelopment.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.snippet: Next-generation sequencing of 2 individuals with MCDR3-linked NCMD revealed a 900-kb duplication that included the entire IRX1 gene (V5).
Next-generation sequencing of 2 individuals with MCDR3-linked NCMD revealed a 900-kb duplication that included the entire IRX1 gene (V5).

nodes.description: More severe NCMD lesions can show large central thinning or excavation with loss of macular tissue, clinically resembling atrophic or coloboma-like macular malformation.

evidence.snippet: OCT revealed varying degrees of macular structure disorganization.

nodes.description: Grade 1 or mild NCMD can present with drusen-like macular lesions.

evidence.snippet: The 80-year-old mother of the proband had drusen-like lesions consistent with grade 1 NCMD.

nodes.description: NCMD is a congenital or early-onset macular dystrophy with variable lesion severity. Mild disease can resemble macular drusen, whereas severe disease can present with large coloboma-like macular malformations.

evidence.snippet: All three patients had macular involvement ranging from patchy yellowish-white lesions to big-area thinning, which are typical for NCMD.
This variant lies in a DNase 1 hypersensitivity site (DHS) upstream of both the PRDM13 and CCNC genes.
A complete duplication of the PRDM13 gene was also discovered in a single family (V4).

A novel 134.6 kb (g.99932464-100067110dup) tandem duplication on chromosome 6 (NC_000006.11) encompassing the entire CCNC and PRDM13 genes and a DNase 1 hypersensitivity site in the MCDR1 locus was identified.
This suggests that this site, chr6:99593030, is a mutational hotspot.

nodes.description: Central visual acuity is variably affected. Some affected individuals have relatively preserved acuity, whereas severe lesions or complications can cause substantial central vision loss.

evidence.snippet: The proband presented with lifelong bilateral vision impairment with bilaterally symmetric vitelliform Best disease-like appearing macular lesions.
